_schema: Each case: id, difficulty (easy|medium|hard|trap), question, plus any of the constraint fields documented in evaluator.py (expected, expected_any, expected_exactly + candidate_pool, expected_none, must_refuse, expected_page). Add your own cases here, or pass a different file with `--testset path.json`. Traps (must_refuse / expected_none) are what stop the harness from trivially scoring 100% — keep them.

cases.question: What is the USB port output current?
What is the tallest object I can place underneath the desk?
How many people are needed to assemble the desk?
How do I reset the desk?
How do I lock the control panel display?
What is the maximum height limit range I can set the desk to?
What is the difference between Jog and Continuation mode?
To lower the desk, which single key do you press: up or down?
What is the warranty period for this desk?
How much does this standing desk cost?
How do I pair the desk with Bluetooth on my phone?
What is the USB port output voltage, in volts?
What is the total vertical height-adjustment range of the desk, in centimeters (a single number)?
cases._note: KNOWN FAILURE: total travel = 120cm ceiling - 72cm floor = 48cm. The model gives the bands (72-120) or computes wrong (24cm) but does not reliably produce 48. Documents a multi-hop numeric-reasoning limitation.

cases.question: List every error code that relates to the motor.
cases._note: HARD but PASSES: exhaustive enumeration. Interesting contrast with fail-error-code-count below — the model can list all the codes but cannot count them.

cases.question: How many distinct error codes are listed in the error code table in total?
cases._note: KNOWN FAILURE: there are 20 codes (E01-E08, HOT, E11-E18, E40-E43). The model lists all 20 correctly but miscounts the total as 24 - a genuine counting/aggregation limitation.
